@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "coredns-1519-avahi-bonjour-mdns-plugin-for-coredns",
+  "missionClass": "fixer",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "coredns: avahi / Bonjour / mDNS plugin for CoreDNS?",
+    "description": "avahi / Bonjour / mDNS plugin for CoreDNS?. Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current coredns deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your coredns version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns\nhelm list -n coredns 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working coredns installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review coredns configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant coredns configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns\nkubectl get configmap -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=coredns\n```\nI am using Avahi on a Raspberry Pi (running Hypriot) to advertise a hostname.local on the local network."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for avahi / Bonjour / mDNS plugin for CoreDNS?",
+        "description": "Or maybe not for one-shot queries. Sigh. I can't look into it more now, maybe tomorrow.\n\n```\n   The most basic kind of Multicast DNS client may simply send standard\n   DNS queries blindly to 224.0.0.251:5353, without necessarily even\n   being aware of what a multicast address is.  This change can\n \n```yaml\n$ dns-sd -G v4 idaeus.local\nDATE: ---Sat 10 Feb 2018---\n14:55:14.640  ...STARTING...\nTimestamp     A/R Flags if Hostname                               Address                                      TTL\n14:55:14.837  Add     2  4 idaeus.local.                          10.10.10.192                                 120\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns\nkubectl get events -n coredns --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"avahi / Bonjour / mDNS plugin for CoreDNS?\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Or maybe not for one-shot queries. Sigh. I can't look into it more now, maybe tomorrow.\n\n```\n   The most basic kind of Multicast DNS client may simply send standard\n   DNS queries blindly to 192.0.2.1:5353, without necessarily even\n   being aware of what a multicast address is.  This change can\n   typically be implemented with just a few lines of code in an existing\n   DNS resolver library.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "$ dns-sd -G v4 idaeus.local\nDATE: ---Sat 10 Feb 2018---\n14:55:14.640  ...STARTING...\nTimestamp     A/R Flags if Hostname                               Address                                      TTL\n14:55:14.837  Add     2  4 idaeus.local.                          10.10.10.192                                 120",
+        "mdns {\n     ...\n  }",
+        ".:53 {\n log\n forward local 192.0.2.1:5353 {\n\thealth_check 0\n\tmax_fails 0\n }\n proxy . /etc/resolv.conf\n}"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
